item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I found it ok with a bit of mystery. My only problems were mentioned in other people's comments.I think it has a lot of potentials and with some revisions, it can become more popular. This seems like an SCP to link back to when I write an SCP that could relate to another SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels dissatisfying. Essentially, it's a room that causes itching above a corpse that kills people by teleporting locusts into them…

The method of killing seems inefficient and easy to avoid; if the locusts demanifest outside of SCP-4595, people just shouldn't be inside the room when working around SCP-4595-A. (Of course vapor in the lungs isn't healthy, but survivable, depending on temperature.)

Last but not least, i doubt ground penetrating radar works as described here. Sure it isn't supposed to be sonar? Nonetheless, in neither way it should be possible to determine the stage of decomposition of a body this way, not to speak of facial wounds.

How do they know she's dead anyway? Leaving out the possibilty she could still be alive seems to add creepyness, and it seems this is what you were going for.

Overall, this looks like an ineffectual Thing That Kills You to me, and the backstory isn't captivating to make up for it. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't see what's the point of this SCP is besides the creep factor. - 1 for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is mystery to it but nothing special. Feels like a well written Series I article. No vote I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, with hope that future articles from yourself and/or other authors will link back to this and perhaps expand upon its mystery.

I don't know Kaktus, with your amazing backlog this just seems lazy in comparison. There are a number of sentences that I find unpolished, there's little narrative, and frankly I find this just not up to par on Series V standards. I understand you wanting to take a break from your usual Epics, but I just don't find this compelling. Am I missing something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta say, I'm usually not a fan of these short kind of articles, but this one delivers a lot of very nice, creepy imagery. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, I feel like I am missing something but not in a way that makes me want to solve the mystery? No vote from me

item.title: Re: the WITCH
item.content:encoded: For reference, the image comes from here and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki’s license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this could just as easily have been a big ole thing again, but I also appreciate the concise, easily navigated format of the current article, so +1 with the hopes of seeing more some day

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is a cool little SCP :3

there's a few things i have questions about:

  • the first sentence feels irrelevant considering the second sentence supersedes it? like, either no personnel are allowed to enter SCP-4595 or only authorised personnel are?
  • the idea that ground penetrating radar can detect wounds on a dead body feels, wrong to me, but i don't know if this is just my lack of knowledge of how accurate it is?

EDIT: actually, i've thought about this more and "Many subjects who are exposed to SCP-4595" feels weird to me too, both in that it's imprecise as to how likely the effects are to occur and because the many implies the Foundation has sent like, at least 30 or 40 people into SCP-4595 to test it's effect for no particular reason?

item.title: the WITCH
item.content:encoded:
If you can believe it, this was once a big ole thing. Decided it didn't need to be a big ole thing, and now it's a small ole thing.

I hope you enjoy it!
